mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in total nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates robust labor demand and rising household income, which supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The fraction of positive forward 20-day returns when PAYEMS has increased in the prior month is not significantly above 0.5 (binomial test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production (INDPRO) declines for two consecutive months, it signals the onset of a manufacturing contraction that reduces corporate profits and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month change negative for two consecutive months
failure_condition: The average forward 60-day return following consecutive monthly declines in INDPRO is not negative or has a t-statistic > -1.0.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) indicate broad-based consumer and investment demand strength, which boosts cyclical earnings and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
candidate_trigger: Both HOUST and RSAFS increase month-over-month in the same month
failure_condition: The joint condition does not produce a mean 5-day forward return above zero with a 95% confidence interval excluding zero.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of low inflation (CPI below 2% year-on-year), a rise in industrial production (INDPRO) represents real output growth without upward pressure on input costs or discount rates, which positively surprises equity markets over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In subsamples where CPIAUCSL YoY < 2%, the correlation between INDPRO change and forward 20-day returns is not positive (Pearson r <= 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) is at or below 1%, it indicates an accommodative monetary stance that compresses the cost of capital and pushes equity valuations higher, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS <= 1%, the mean forward 20-day return is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: The real interest rate (FEDFUNDS minus year-on-year CPI inflation) drives equity returns because negative real rates encourage borrowing and asset allocation away from cash, whereas positive real rates tighten financial conditions; a negative real rate predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: Real rate (FEDFUNDS - CPIAUCSL YoY) is negative
failure_condition: The average forward 60-day return when real rate is negative is not higher than when it is positive (difference < 0, bootstrapped p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A rising real GDP (GDPC1) reflects accelerating aggregate demand and economic expansion, which translates into higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter, as corporate fundamentals improve in step with output growth.
failure_condition: The Rank IC between quarterly GDPC1 growth and subsequent 60-day returns is below 0.05 over any 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When monthly retail sales (RSAFS) growth exceeds 1%, it signals a consumption boost that quickly feeds into investor sentiment and high-frequency equity returns, producing a positive S&P 500 forward return over the next week.
failure_condition: When RSAFS MoM growth > 1%, the mean forward 5-day return is not positive (t-test p > 0.05).
